vulnerability.reference: Scientists and regulators trust AI-optimized NMR readings without cross-validation, enabling persistent bias.
vulnerability.generated: Environmental scientists lack field calibration references to detect simulation-trained pulse sequence biases under real soil heterogeneity.

impact.reference: Incorrect soil carbon stock estimates undermine climate mitigation policies and carbon market transactions.
impact.generated: Biased carbon-13 estimates undermine soil carbon accounting, misdirecting agricultural practices and carbon credit markets.

dose_response.reference: RL-trained pulse sequences mis-characterize spin relaxation, enabling biased carbon-13 estimates, which when trusted without cross-validation by environmental scientists, leads to incorrect stock estimates undermining climate policies and carbon markets.
dose_response.generated: Simulation-trained RL pulse sequences mis-characterize spin relaxation under real low-field conditions, distorting quantification, and through deployment by environmental scientists lacking field calibration references to detect biases under soil heterogeneity, triggers undetected carbon-13 estimate errors, leading to biased estimates that undermine soil carbon accounting and misdirect agricultural practices and carbon credit markets.
